codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 6 security hardening items (C1, C2, C4, F2, F4, F5). The TOCTOU fix uses Prisma $transaction with a conditional updateMany WHERE status=DRAFT, which correctly serializes concurrent writes at the row-level lock in PostgreSQL Read Committed isolation. The addContentTypeParser removal, brandId null guards, seed password env var, validation bounds, and ADMIN brandId logic are all correctly implemented. 16 new tests cover all fixes including a well-designed concurrent mint race condition test. All existing tests updated for the new category enum values. Code is clean, consistent, and follows existing conventions.

issues.description: ADMIN create with a non-existent brandId will trigger a Prisma P2003 foreign key constraint error resulting in a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a user-friendly 400. The error handler only catches P2002 (unique constraint). Consider adding a P2003 catch or validating brand existence before creating the product.

PRODUCT_CATEGORIES array is duplicated identically in create.ts and update.ts. Could be extracted to a shared constant (e.g., in a common products constants file or in @galileo/shared) to avoid divergence risk.

Security hardening tests were originally written without X-Galileo-Client headers on POST/PATCH requests. The later CSRF feature (commit 1ba30f8) patched these tests, but this means the feature's own test file wasn't self-sufficient at commit time. Not a blocking issue since the tests were updated correctly by the subsequent feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The PRODUCT_CATEGORIES enum array ['Leather Goods','Jewelry','Watches','Fashion','Accessories','Other'] is defined inline in both create.ts and update.ts. AGENTS.md documents the validation bounds requirement (F4) but doesn't document the canonical category values or where they should live. If a future feature adds/changes categories, both files must be updated in sync.

The brandId null guard pattern (check user.role !== 'ADMIN' && !user.brandId → 403) is repeated verbatim in 6 route files. AGENTS.md mentions C4 as a finding but doesn't document the guard as a convention or suggest it should be middleware. Future routes may omit it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Identical guard blocks in: list.ts, create.ts, get.ts, update.ts, mint.ts, qr.ts. Compare to requireRole() which is already a reusable middleware.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The MintError class is introduced as an ad-hoc error pattern in mint.ts for transaction error handling. This is a reasonable pattern but not documented. Other routes use direct reply.status().send() for errors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/api/src/routes/products/mint.ts:127-137 defines MintError class, used to throw from within $transaction and catch outside.

summary: PASS. All 6 security hardening items are correctly implemented: (C1) Mint TOCTOU fixed with atomic $transaction + conditional updateMany, (C2) global addContentTypeParser removed, (C4) brandId null guard added to all 6 product routes with all brandId! assertions removed, (F2) seed password uses SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var with production guard and dev fallback, (F4) validation bounds added to create and update schemas, (F5) ADMIN brandId logic correctly requires body.brandId and non-ADMIN ignores it. 16 new tests cover all scenarios including concurrent mint race condition. Three non-blocking issues noted: missing P2003 error handling for ADMIN create with invalid brandId, duplicated PRODUCT_CATEGORIES constant, and test CSRF header dependency on later commit.
